candidate_summary: Multi-stage tribunal-style LLM evaluation with elder-judge panel, chair-elected coordinator, evidence-anchored rubrics, sanction continuum (peace-oath / revise / reject / marime-blacklist), and restoration-path reapplication; analogized from Romani kris customary court (krisnitori elders, marime ritual impurity ostracism, oath-to-ancestors, public verdict).

results.name: Rubric-Based Evals LLM-as-Judge Methodologies 2026 (Masood Medium)
results.description: Recursive rubric decomposition + Anthropic 2026 panel-based LLM judges + anchor-example calibration.
results.rationale: Panel-based LLM judges + recursive rubric decomposition + anchor-example calibration directly maps to the elder-judge panel + evidence-anchored rubric core of KRIS-TRIBUNAL. Missing the sanction continuum and marime-blacklist + restoration-path. Strong functional overlap on the panel+rubric+anchor sub-mechanism.

results.name: AgentsCourt Agent-as-Judge LLM Evaluation (arXiv 2508.02994)
results.description: AgentsCourt simulated legal-case agent judges + fallacy injection + tribunal-style evaluation.
results.rationale: Exact-twin overlap — AgentsCourt is explicitly a tribunal/court-style multi-agent LLM evaluation simulation. This directly instantiates the multi-stage tribunal + multi-judge core of KRIS-TRIBUNAL. The sanction continuum / marime-blacklist / restoration-path additions are not described, but the central tribunal-with-judges mechanism is the same artefact with the same effect on LLM evaluation.

results.name: Calibrate Don't Curate Label-Efficient Noisy LLM Judges (arXiv 2605.09702)
results.description: Calibration-based bias correction + confidence intervals for LLM judges; item response theory.
results.rationale: Concerns LLM judge calibration + IRT for noisy judges. Touches the judge-reliability/coordinator aspect but does not implement a tribunal procedure, sanction continuum, or restoration-path. Functional overlap is partial — calibrated judges only.

results.name: Survey LLM-as-a-Judge (arXiv 2411.15594)
results.description: Comprehensive survey of LLM-as-Judge — panels, juries, rubrics, calibration.
results.rationale: Survey explicitly covers panels, juries, rubrics, and calibration — i.e., the design space of KRIS-TRIBUNAL's panel-rubric-coordinator components. Surveys typically saturate the functional space even if no single artefact instantiates the sanction continuum or restoration-path. Crosses functional 0.7 by covering jury + panel + rubric subspaces.

results.name: Constitutional AI Harmlessness AI Feedback (arXiv 2212.08073)
results.description: CAI generate→critique→revise loop with constitutional principles.
results.rationale: Constitutional AI generate→critique→revise loop directly maps to the multi-stage trial procedure with revision/restoration-path. Single-cycle, single-critic (no multi-judge panel + chair election + sanction continuum), but the critique-revise-restoration core is the same artefact + effect. Sits exactly at the v5 functional threshold.

results.name: RULERS Locked Rubrics Evidence-Anchored Robust LLM (arXiv 2601.08654)
results.description: Locked rubrics + evidence-anchored scoring for robust LLM evaluation.
results.rationale: RULERS locked rubrics + evidence-anchored scoring IS the evidence-anchored rubric assessment phase of KRIS-TRIBUNAL — exact twin on that component. Missing tribunal procedure + sanction continuum, but the candidate explicitly lists RULERS as its closest twin, confirming same-artefact same-effect on the rubric-anchoring component.

results.name: Multi-Turn LLM Evaluation 2026 Confident AI
results.description: Multi-turn pipeline evaluation + regression testing + continuous monitoring + thresholds.
results.rationale: Multi-turn pipeline evaluation with regression + thresholds shares the multi-stage pipeline-integration aspect, but lacks the panel-of-judges, rubric-anchoring, sanction continuum, blacklist, and restoration-path mechanisms. Below both semantic and functional thresholds.

results.name: C3AI Constitutional AI Constitutions WWW 2025 (10.1145/3696410.3714705)
results.description: Framework for crafting + evaluating constitutions for CAI evaluation.
results.rationale: C3AI framework for crafting constitutions covers the principled-evaluation aspect (constitutions as rubric-like governing principles) but does not instantiate tribunal procedure, multi-judge panel, sanction continuum, marime-blacklist, or restoration-path. Crosses semantic 0.7 just at the threshold; functional below 0.7.

decision_rationale: Seven of eight results are independently hits under the v5 rubric (kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7). The candidate's core artefact — multi-judge LLM panel + evidence-anchored rubrics + critique-revise loop — is saturated by prior work (Constitutional AI critique-revise, RULERS locked-rubrics + evidence-anchoring, AgentsCourt tribunal-style agent judges, rubric-based LLM-as-Judge panel methodologies, the LLM-as-Judge survey, calibrated noisy-judge IRT, and C3AI constitutions). The novel additions (chair-elected coordinator, sanction continuum with marime-blacklist, restoration-path reapplication) are surface-level recombinations of standard panel-voting + threshold-gating + revision-loop mechanisms already in the field. With 7/8 hits and ExactTwin functional matches at AgentsCourt (0.90) and the rubric-panel methodology (0.85), the candidate fails the novelty bar.
